message:
test(demo_showcase): comprehensive unit tests for domain action API

- Tests for create/start/cancel/retry/complete/fail job lifecycle
- Tests for notification severity matching (info vs warning vs error)
- Tests for log entry levels and content verification
- Tests for alert creation on failures
- Tests for deployment full lifecycle with rollback scenarios
- Tests for ID generator sequential behavior
- Tests for ActionResult merging and command batching
- 32 total action API tests with high coverage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1nwu","title":"Settings: toggles (mouse, animations, no-color, syntax)","description":"Expose runtime toggles:
- mouse on/off
- animations on/off
- force ASCII/no color
- syntax highlighting + line numbers (if enabled)

Implementation notes:
- This page should mutate the live app state, but also reflect the initial defaults from `demo_showcase::Config`.

Acceptance:
- Toggling updates app behavior live without restart.
- Toggles are discoverable and have immediate, visible effects (not hidden flags).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:01.250474646Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:29:20.250220945Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","settings"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-274o","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:12:01.272885316Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:54.907924170Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:28:38.554374326Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-2szb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:00.214777601Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1nwu","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:21:02.860339836Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1o6y","title":"Implement huh Multiselect, Text, FilePicker, Note field types","description":"Implement missing Huh field types: Multiselect, FilePicker, and Note. (Textarea is tracked separately under bd-212m.4.1.)

Scope:
- Add field structs + state for multiselect, filepicker, note in crates/huh/src/lib.rs.
- Implement update/view logic and keybindings consistent with Go.
- Add validation hooks and theming support for each field.
- Expand conformance fixtures and add unit tests for each field type.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Conformance fixtures for multiselect, text, filepicker, and note pass with no skips.
- Unit tests cover input handling, selection toggles, and rendering for each field.
- FEATURE_PARITY.md updated to reflect field coverage.","notes":"Textarea scope moved to bd-212m.4.1 to keep parity gap tracking focused.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:22.524453153Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T01:33:37.237925265Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T01:19:31.436260994Z","close_reason":"Implemented Text and FilePicker fields. MultiSelect and Note were already present. All field types have builder pattern, validation, keyboard handling, theme support, and view rendering.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1oe0","title":"demo_showcase core: optionally use bubbletea derive macro for one model","description":"Optional showcase: use `bubbletea-macros` derive for *one* small model/component inside demo_showcase.

Why:
- It demonstrates that the workspace’s proc-macro ergonomics actually work in a real app.
- It also gives users a concrete reference for when the derive is worth using.

Scope:
- Pick a small, isolated model (examples):
  - a tiny counter widget in Settings/About
  - a modal state machine (open/close) with a few fields
- Do NOT macro-derive the entire App (keep core architecture explicit and readable).

Acceptance:
- The derived model compiles cleanly and behaves correctly.
- The code includes a brief doc comment explaining why derive was used here.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:52.572059183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:27:58.105847511Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","demo_showcase","macros"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1oe0","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:52.588543294Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1oe0","depends_on_id":"bd-cakm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:30.485956680Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- start job -> job exists + correct initial state
- progress ticks update job correctly
- cancel job -> state transitions + logs/notifications
- retry failed job -> new attempt semantics

Logging:

 {"id":"bd-1pdg","title":"Glow: truncate_string handles zero width","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-25T16:11:52.549379559Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T16:25:02.162307739Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T16:25:02.161950406Z","close_reason":"Completed: handle zero-width truncate_string; add tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1qwj","title":"demo_showcase core: notifications/toasts + status messages","description":"Add transient notifications and persistent status messaging.

Why:
- Real apps communicate state changes (saved, error, connected, job finished).

Implementation:
- Small toast stack or one-line status area.
- Uses lipgloss styles (success/warn/error) and optional harmonica animation later.

Acceptance:
- Pages can emit notifications via app messages; app renders them consistently.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:01.374394082Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:25.545173990Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","polish","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1qwj","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:08:01.402524089Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qwj","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:25.545146309Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1qwj","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:24.293003527Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1tdh","title":"Jobs: paginator integration for long lists","description":"Add pagination for long Jobs lists using `bubbles::paginator`.

Why:
- Long lists are a common real-world case; pagination prevents the UI from becoming sluggish.
- It also showcases another Bubbles component in a realistic workflow.

Behavior:
- Paginator reflects total pages and current page.
- Works correctly with filtering/sorting:
  - applying a filter updates total pages
  - selection remains stable (best-effort) when the filtered set changes
- Keyboard:
  - next/prev page shortcuts
  - jump to first/last (optional)

Acceptance:
- Large job sets remain responsive.
- Pagination UX feels consistent with the rest of the app (styled, discoverable).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.084401297Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T23:28:43.856579389Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","jobs","paginator"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-1dr6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:14:21.103425435Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1tdh","depends_on_id":"bd-3rwl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:19:02.336132860Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
